stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest LLM releases.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core field of NLP/LLMs, specifically using the 'text-generation' pipeline.
The model is associated with major industry players (Meta/Llama) and standard ML formats (transformers, safetensors).

stories.title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is committing massive investment ($1T) to bolster its semiconductor and AI capabilities.
The national strategy aims to establish a leadership position in physical AI and commercial humanoid robotics by 2028.
The focus highlights a strategic pivot toward advanced hardware and embodied AI applications.

stories.title: Google warns EU's plans to weaken its monopoly could expose user data
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is raising privacy concerns regarding the EU's regulatory push.
The EU is mandating that Google share search data and open up Android AI to competitors.
The conflict highlights the tension between antitrust enforcement and user data privacy.

stories.title: SWE-INTERACT: Reimagining SWE Benchmarks as User-Driven Long-Horizon Coding Sessions
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: Introduces SWE-Interact, a novel testbed for evaluating coding AI agents.
Shifts evaluation from autonomous, upfront-requirement tasks to realistic, multi-turn, user-driven workflows.
Simulates a developer interaction where requirements are revealed progressively by a user simulator.

stories.title: Scaling the Horizon, Not the Parameters: Reaching Trillion-Parameter Performance with a 35B Agent
stories.author: Yuhao Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces Agents-A1, a 35B Mixture-of-Experts Agentic Model.
Achieves trillion-parameter-level performance not by increasing model size, but by scaling the agent horizon.
Utilizes a long-horizon knowledge-action infrastructure to manage complex, long-trajectory tasks (average length of 45K steps).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in faster response times for language models.
The updated engine also yields higher quality model outputs and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these personal AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It integrates native support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating active development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows directly to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: US offers $10 million for info on group behind Signal and WhatsApp hacking spree
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: US government is offering a $10 million reward for information regarding the hackers targeting Signal and WhatsApp.
The cyber operation is attributed to two distinct Russia-state-sponsored groups.
The hacking activity has reportedly been active since at least March.

stories.title: VLK: Learning Humanoid Loco-Manipulation from Synthetic Interactions in Reconstructed Scenes
stories.author: Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the critical bottleneck in humanoid robotics: generating synchronized vision-language-kinematics (VLK) data.
Proposes a novel synthetic data generation pipeline utilizing 3D Gaussian Splatting to create complex training environments.
Aims to enable perception-based humanoid loco-manipulation by bridging egocentric observations, language commands, and whole-body motion.

stories.title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
stories.author: Yang Deng
bullet_points: Introduces WorldEvolver, a framework for self-evolving world models to enhance LLM agent planning.
Addresses the issue of unreliable foresight in LLM agents by allowing the world model to revise its context during deployment.
Maintains the downstream agent and core model parameters frozen while improving decision-making via integrated modules like Episodic Memory.

stories.title: Words Speak Louder Than Code: Investigating Cognitive Heuristics in LLM-Based Code Vulnerability Detection
stories.author: Z. Berkay Celik
bullet_points: Investigates the influence of human cognitive heuristics on LLMs used for code vulnerability detection.
Addresses a gap in research by systematically exploring how biases affect LLM security assessments.
Presents a controlled framework for analyzing LLM performance in identifying code vulnerabilities.

stories.title: One-Step Gradient Delay is Not a Barrier for Large-Scale Asynchronous Pipeline Parallel LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Samuel Horváth
bullet_points: Addresses the inefficiency of synchronous Pipeline Parallelism in large-scale LLM pretraining by introducing asynchronous methods.
PipeDream-2BW is highlighted as a novel asynchronous scheduling technique that maintains a constant one-step gradient delay.
The research aims to maximize GPU throughput in LLM training by mitigating pipeline bubbles caused by asynchronous operations.

stories.title: Self-Evolving World Models for LLM Agent Planning
stories.author: Yang Deng
bullet_points: Introduces WorldEvolver, a framework for self-evolving world models to enhance LLM agent planning.
Addresses the issue of unreliable foresight in LLM agents by revising deployment-time context without altering core model parameters.
Integrates three key modules, including Episodic Memory, to provide agents with predictive capabilities for long-horizon tasks.

stories.title: Zluda 6 release (run unmodified CUDA applications on non-Nvidia GPUs)
stories.author: Tiberium
bullet_points: Zluda 6 is a new release enabling the execution of unmodified CUDA applications on non-Nvidia GPUs.
This technology significantly lowers the barrier to entry for using high-performance computing resources outside the Nvidia ecosystem.
It represents a major step toward GPU hardware agnosticism in the AI/ML and scientific computing fields.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for more complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing the model's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to include local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: This motor could be the future of e-bikes
stories.author: Thomas Ricker
bullet_points: New e-bike motor technology promises automated gear adjustment based on user-selected pedaling cadence.
The system aims to eliminate the need for traditional, fragile derailleur or heavy multi-speed cassettes.
It offers flexibility, allowing users to choose between automated assistance or manual control.

stories.title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music, but isn’t banning it outright
stories.author: Andrew Liszewski
bullet_points: Tidal is implementing new policies regarding AI-generated music rather than an outright ban.
Starting July 15th, Tidal will label tracks identified as 100% AI-generated with a specific icon.
Currently, AI-generated tracks on Tidal are not monetizable, aligning with the platform's stated goal to 'protect artists'.

stories.title: Sony’s next-gen PlayStation will go ‘beyond the living room’
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Sony is planning a next-generation PlayStation experience that extends beyond the traditional living room.
The announcement suggests Sony is addressing the migration of users to gaming PCs.
The platform aims to offer new ways for users to engage with PlayStation games outside of a standard console setup.

stories.title: US renewable boom passes key milestone in April
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: US renewable energy generation has reached a significant milestone.
Small-scale solar power played a crucial role in this growth.
Renewable energy output nearly tripled coal's generation in the US.

stories.title: Supreme Court ruling guts government’s use of geofence warrants
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The Supreme Court addressed the legality of government use of geofence warrants.
The ruling did not definitively declare geofence warrants unconstitutional.
This decision impacts surveillance practices and digital privacy rights.

stories.title: LeVo 2: Stable and Melodious Song Generation via Hierarchical Representation Modeling and Progressive Post-Training
stories.author: Dong Yu
bullet_points: Introduces LeVo 2, a hybrid LLM-Diffusion framework designed for controllable, full-length song generation.
Addresses a key trade-off in existing systems: balancing vocal-instrument coordination with detailed acoustic rendering.
Aims to overcome limitations in current models regarding coherence, musicality, and adherence to lyrics/prompts.

stories.title: GROW$^2$: Grounding Which and Where for Robot Tool Use
stories.author: David Hsu
bullet_points: The research addresses the 'open-world affordance grounding' problem in robotics, allowing robots to use tools creatively beyond their intended design.
GROW$^2$ (GROunding Which and Where) is introduced as a new method to solve this by leveraging object parts as a hierarchical abstraction.
The core challenge involves enabling robots to select an appropriate object and precisely localize the region where the tool action should occur (e.g., using a plate to cut a cake).

stories.title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
stories.author: Divya Chaudhary
bullet_points: The paper challenges the common assumption that conservative offline training prevents reward hacking during online adaptation in reasoning models.
It empirically and mechanistically investigates how different levels of conservatism ($\beta$) affect policy behavior.
The study uses a Qwen3-14B policy trained via Direct Preference Optimisation (DPO) to test the limits of safe policy initialization.

stories.title: C$^{2}$R: Cross-sample Consistency Regularization Mitigates Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Defu Lian
bullet_points: Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) are crucial for interpreting large language models by extracting human-understandable features.
Scaling SAEs to large dictionaries suffers from two major problems: feature splitting (fragmenting concepts) and feature absorption (creating arbitrary exceptions).
The paper introduces C²R (Cross-sample Consistency Regularization) to mitigate these latent reliability issues.

stories.title: MESA: Prioritizing Vulnerable Communication Channels for Securing Multi-Agent Systems
stories.author: Patrick McDaniel
bullet_points: The paper addresses security vulnerabilities in Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) communication channels.
It proposes a method (MESA) to prioritize limited security resources based on potential attack impact.
The research highlights that the impact of channel-level attacks in MAS is highly non-uniform.

stories.title: Pessimism's Paradox: Conservative Offline Training Amplifies Reward Hacking During Online Adaptation in Reasoning Models
stories.author: Divya Chaudhary
bullet_points: The paper challenges the common assumption that conservative offline training prevents reward hacking during online adaptation.
It empirically and mechanistically investigates how different levels of conservatism ($\beta$) affect policy behavior in reasoning models (specifically using Qwen3-14B).
The research suggests that conservative training might paradoxically amplify reward hacking when models adapt online.

stories.title: C$^{2}$R: Cross-sample Consistency Regularization Mitigates Feature Splitting and Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders
stories.author: Defu Lian
bullet_points: The paper addresses critical limitations in Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) when applied to large language models (LLMs).
Key problems identified are 'feature splitting' (fragmentation of concepts) and 'feature absorption' (creation of arbitrary exceptions).
The proposed solution, C²R (Cross-sample Consistency Regularization), aims to improve latent reliability in SAEs.

stories.title: Wireless Backdoor Attack and Defense for Semantic Communications over Multiple Access Channel
stories.author: Sennur Ulukus
bullet_points: The research addresses security vulnerabilities in Semantic Communication (SemCom) systems operating over multiple access channels.
It specifically investigates the threat of 'Wireless Backdoor Attacks' targeting multi-user semantic inference.
The paper proposes defense mechanisms for SemCom systems where multiple transmitters share a common receiver.

stories.title: A Hybrid Framework For Crypto-Ransomware Detection In Enterprise Shared Storage
stories.author: Mohammad Jabed Morshed Chowdhury
bullet_points: The research addresses the evolving threat of crypto-ransomware targeting enterprise shared storage, moving beyond traditional endpoint focus.
The paper proposes a 'Hybrid Framework' specifically designed to detect ransomware activity within shared network drives.
This work is relevant as traditional endpoint detection mechanisms are insufficient against attacks that propagate across shared corporate storage.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Generation and Decision-Making Under Ambiguity
stories.author: Iryna Gurevych
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical need for trust and reliability in Large Language Models (LLMs) when applied to complex, subjective real-world tasks.
It focuses specifically on uncertainty-aware decision-making algorithms, an area less explored compared to model training improvements.
The research presents and evaluates various methods to help models quantify and manage their own uncertainty during decision-making.

stories.title: Attractor States Emerge in Multi-Turn LLM Conversations
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Investigates the long-term dynamics of multi-agent LLM conversations to see if they settle into stable 'attractor states'.
The study tests this behavior across 7 different LLMs using 20 controversial topics in both self-play and mixed-play debates.
Analysis tracks conversational trajectories using representation space, discourse traits, and stated stances.

stories.title: Uncertainty-Aware Generation and Decision-Making Under Ambiguity
stories.author: Iryna Gurevych
bullet_points: Addresses the critical need for trust and reliability in complex LLM applications.
Focuses on uncertainty-aware decision-making algorithms, moving beyond just model training.
Relevant to real-world tasks where outputs are subjective and require confidence metrics.

stories.title: Attractor States Emerge in Multi-Turn LLM Conversations
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: Investigates the long-run dynamics of multi-turn LLM conversations, a poorly understood area in multi-agent settings.
Tests the hypothesis that LLM discussions settle into 'attractor-like' stable behaviors across various topics and models.
Compares self-play and mixed-play debates across 7 LLMs using representation space and discourse traits.

stories.title: Morphing into Hybrid Attention Models
stories.author: Yu Cheng
bullet_points: The research focuses on hybrid attention models to boost long-context efficiency in NLP.
These models achieve efficiency by substituting full-attention layers with linear attention.
A key challenge identified is that current layer selection methods use heuristics and fail to capture inter-layer dependencies.

stories.title: Poller: Are LLMs Suitable for Evaluating the Poetry Understanding Task?
stories.author: Lidia S. Chao
bullet_points: Proposes 'Poller,' a novel method using LLMs to evaluate poetry understanding.
Addresses the limitations of traditional automatic evaluation methods for Chinese poetry.
Aims to provide a scalable alternative to expensive human evaluation for literary tasks.

stories.title: SIMAX: A Scalable and Interpretable Framework for Multi-Fidelity and Annotated Clinician-Patient Dialogue Simulation
stories.author: Chuan Hong
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability and cost issues associated with manually coding large volumes of clinician-patient dialogue data.
Introduces SIMAX, a framework designed for multi-fidelity and annotated simulation of clinical conversations.
Aims to enable AI-driven communication coding systems by providing a scalable method to generate necessary training data.

stories.title: Situation Perception: A Necessary Primitive to Artificial Superintelligence
stories.author: Jaymari Chua
bullet_points: The paper argues that current LLMs, despite their advanced pattern recognition, lack true general intelligence.
It posits that 'Situation Perception'—understanding concepts like object permanence and causality—is a necessary primitive for Artificial Superintelligence.
The research contrasts LLM statistical mastery with the foundational, embodied learning processes of human infants.

stories.title: European digital ID wallets rely on safety services of Google and Apple
stories.author: donohoe
bullet_points: European digital ID wallets are integrating safety services from Google and Apple.
This indicates a reliance on major tech players for security infrastructure in digital identity.
The story points to a significant trend in the standardization and reliance on established tech giants for critical identity functions.
